pi_items.source: setup_sumo.sh script which creates this directory and copies data into it within the environment
setup_task.sh and setup_sumo.sh scripts which initialize and clean this specific directory
export_result.sh explicitly checks for and copies this file to evaluate the modified network
export_result.sh explicitly checks for and copies this file to evaluate simulation metrics
export_result.sh explicitly checks for and copies this textual report for the verifier to parse

privileged_info_summary: The task relies on the real-world SUMO dataset for Bologna Acosta (acosta_buslanes.net.xml). By programmatically parsing the original network XML, it is verified that the busiest junction matching the lexicographical tie-breaker is ID '18', which features exactly 4 incoming edges. At junction '18', there are exactly 6 incoming connections: 2 are left turns (dir='l') and 4 are other turns. A successful execution requires modifying the network to fully eliminate these 2 left turns (leaving 4 total connections at junction '18') and generating a valid textual report mapping these figures.
